items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Mahila Co-operative Bank Ltd., Bangalore, Karnataka
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 11, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹5 lakh (Rupees Five Lakh only) on Mahila Co-operative Bank Ltd., Bangalore, Karnataka (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Loans and advances to directors, their relatives, and firms / concerns in which they are interested’. items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live: Sensex rises over 300 pts, Nifty near 23,950 as markets gain from easing geopolitical risks
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: Benchmark indices continued their upward momentum on Tuesday, helped by lower crude prices and easing geopolitical concerns. Investors tracked global cues, foreign fund flows and signs of recovery after recent pressure from oil-related worries.

items.title: Its ‘deferred, not abandoned’, awaiting right window: SIS on IPO plan for cash logistics biz
items.summary: In the latest annual report, the company said "the intent (for IPO) is unchanged", adding that it has already filed the draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) for SIS Cash Services

items.title: Tata Motors PV confident of delivering industry-leading growth in FY27: Chairman N Chandrasekaran
items.summary: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles anticipates industry-leading growth in FY27. This confidence stems from a strong lineup of new vehicle launches and diverse powertrain options. The company will continue its collaboration with Jaguar Land Rover on manufacturing and technology. Focus remains on safety, sustainability, quality, and customer satisfaction. Tata Motors is committed to Net Zero by 2040.

items.title: Oil prices fall, tanker bosses remain cautious on resumption of Hormuz transit
items.summary: Global oil prices ticked lower on Tuesday as the Iran peace agreement dominated the G7 meeting in France.

items.title: Adani Ports to invest up to $100 million with Kaleris to accelerate terminal automation
items.summary: Adani Ports is partnering with Kaleris to deploy AI-powered systems across its container terminals. This expansion aims to significantly increase cargo handling capacity by 2030. The technology will enhance operational efficiency and customer experience. This digital upgrade supports Adani Ports' ambitious growth plans and its goal to handle one billion tonnes of cargo annually.

items.title: Shipping lines stay cautious even after US-Iran peace deal, await security clarity before resuming Gulf operations
items.summary: The US-Iran peace agreement raises hopes for smoother shipping in the Gulf, yet major lines Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. remain hesitant. The industry awaits essential security assurances before resuming operations as costs surge due to the ongoing maritime conflict.

items.title: Reliance share price extends gains to fourth straight session. Time to buy the stock?
items.summary: Reliance Industries share price has materially underperformed the broader market since January 2026, correcting over 15% as against an 8% decline in Nifty 50, rare divergence for a stock of its benchmark significance during the earnings upgrade cycle.

items.title: AI Investor Rush: Sandisk gains as investors chase AI winners
items.summary: Sandisk surged as investors embraced AI infrastructure growth, benefiting from rising demand for advanced storage solutions. Additional momentum came from SpaceX’s IPO, Western Digital’s stake reduction, and optimism around AI-driven data expansion.

items.title: India's export outlook is challenging for FY27, but worst is over: Elara Securities
items.summary: India's export outlook remains challenging in FY27 amid moderating global demand and elevated electronics imports, but the worst phase appears to be behind as a weaker rupee, resilient services exports and strong remittance inflows are likely to cushion external sector pressures, according to a report by Elara Securities.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech to launch ₹883-crore IPO on Jun 19
items.summary: The price band has been fixed at ₹144-152 per share for the public issue, valuing the company at over ₹4,500 crore at the higher end.

items.title: Hindalco, NALCO to Vedanta Aluminium: Aluminium stocks slide up to 6%. Here's why
items.summary: Shares of aluminium companies face corrections amid a decline in base metal prices. However, Axis Securities maintains BUY ratings for Hindalco and NALCO despite share declines. Analysts forecast aluminium prices to remain between $3,000 and $3,800/t in the short term.

items.title: IFCI shares rally 30% in 3 days, hit fresh record high amid buzz around NSE filing IPO papers by Thursday
items.summary: Vedanta’s Rs 2 lakh crore demerger has sparked renewed interest in its aluminium and power businesses, prompting investors to reassess value creation opportunities. Market focus is shifting towards which segment offers stronger long-term growth, profitability, and sectoral tailwinds as the restructuring reshapes the company’s standalone business prospects and investor positioning.

items.title: Thames Water moves step closer to nationalisation after government objects to rescue deal
items.summary: A government spokesman tells the BBC the current offer "does not do enough to protect consumers or the environment".

items.title: FY27 earnings recovery key to next leg of market upmove: Rajeev Agrawal
items.summary: Global markets are cautiously optimistic about a potential US-Iran agreement easing geopolitical risks and crude oil volatility. However, Indian equities remain restrained, with market experts emphasizing that earnings delivery, particularly for FY27, will be the key driver for future market movements, not just macro triggers.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $66,000 as whale accumulation and institutional buying support sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin holds near $66,000, supported by whale accumulation and institutional buying, with a 0.3% rise in 24 hours. Improving risk sentiment, partly due to a U.S.-Iran peace agreement, fuels buying activity. While institutional demand is supportive, market conviction remains fragile as investors await further macro signals.

items.title: As Europe rearms, 'wingman' aircraft take centre stage
items.summary: Europe and the United States are developing advanced AI-powered drones. These unmanned aircraft, known as wingman drones, will fly alongside fighter jets. Companies like Airbus, Boeing, and General Atomics are showcasing their latest designs. This technology aims to enhance military capabilities and provide a strategic advantage. These drones are expected to be operational in the coming years.

items.title: US-Iran peace deal: Reliance Industries, ONGC; which other oil & gas stocks will emerge as top winners and losers?
items.summary: A US-Iran peace deal is expected to ease oil supply risks, pushing crude prices lower. Nomura sees OMCs, CGDs, and Petronet LNG as key beneficiaries, while upstream firms like ONGC and Oil India may face pressure. Reliance Industries could see moderate downside due to weaker refining margins.

items.title: World's top miners BHP, Rio Tinto see India emerging as steel's next growth frontier beyond China
items.summary: Global mining giants BHP and Rio Tinto are focusing on India for future steel demand. India's rapid urbanisation and infrastructure projects are driving growth. This expansion is expected to offset slowing demand from China. Both companies are well-positioned to support India's ambitious steel production targets. The Global South, particularly India, is becoming a key growth market.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Iran framework signed but not delivered
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump will meet his G7 counterparts as his memorandum of understanding with Iran goes under the microscope with other world leaders.

items.title: India's telecom ARPU to grow 7% CAGR through FY29; narrowing tariff gap with global markets: Report
items.summary: India's telecom sector is set for a multi-year growth phase. Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) is projected to grow at 7% annually from FY26 to FY29. This growth will reduce India's tariff gap with global markets. The sector is poised for stronger cash flows and improved returns.

items.title: India's REIT and InvIT market to attract Rs 11.6 trillion new investments; AUM may double to Rs 20 trillion by 2030: Avendus Capital
items.summary: India’s REIT and InvIT market could attract an additional Rs 11.6 trillion in investments by 2030, taking total assets under management beyond Rs 20 trillion, according to Avendus Capital. Strong participation from mutual funds, insurers, pension funds, and global investors is expected to drive the next phase of growth.

items.title: Private banks remain in focus as credit growth strengthens; selectivity key amid valuation concerns: Dnyanada Vaidya
items.summary: Private sector banks are attracting investors with robust credit growth and stable asset quality. Experts anticipate a rate cycle shift, boosting margins. While some valuation concerns persist, opportunities for rerating exist in select large banks like ICICI and Kotak Mahindra Bank. Deposit mobilization remains a key area to monitor for potential headwinds.

items.title: Australia central bank warns hikes are not off the table as it keeps rates steady at 4.35%
items.summary: In its statement, the RBA said that inflation was "still too high," which warranted to keep the cash rate unchanged

items.title: YES Bank share price rises 3% on partnership with Northern Arc to extend lending offerings
items.summary: YES Bank shares gained after the lender announced a strategic partnership with Northern Arc Capital. The collaboration aims to expand credit access, accelerate digital lending, and provide debt investment opportunities to customers.

items.title: Central banks repatriate gold as global insecurity rises
items.summary: Conflict, sanctions and decline in trust have made the institutions more cautious about storing bullion in other countries

items.title: Small-cap pharma stock hits 52-week high after fundraising move. Details here
items.summary: Sudarshan Pharma share price has gained over 21% in one month and has jumped 56% in three months. The smallcap stock has rallied 47% in six months, and has advanced 24% in one year. Sudarshan Pharma shares have delivered multibagger returns of 400%.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech to launch Rs 883-crore IPO on Jun 19; sets price band at Rs 144-152/share
items.summary: Insurtech firm Turtlemint Fintech Solutions is set to launch its IPO on June 19, aiming to raise up to Rs 883 crore. The offering includes a fresh issue and an offer-for-sale, with proceeds intended for infrastructure, technology teams, and marketing. The company, founded in 2015, simplifies insurance policy purchase and management for customers and advisors.

items.title: Peak XV-backed HomeLane plans India IPO within two years to fuel expansion
items.summary: HomeLane, an Indian home interiors platform, is planning an IPO within 12 to 24 months to fund expansion into new cities and adjacent furnishing categories. The company aims for profitability, projecting significant revenue growth by fiscal 2031, and is investing in AI to enhance design efficiency and reduce costs.

items.title: Rupee inches up, Iran deal longevity worries and hedging drag
items.summary: The Indian rupee saw a modest rise against the U.S. dollar, influenced by a U.S.-Iran truce but tempered by importer hedging and skepticism about the deal's durability. While the peace agreement aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lower oil prices, market participants await further details and anticipate the U.S. Federal Reserve's interest rate decision.

items.title: HDFC Bank to hit dollar bond market under new subsidised scheme: Sources
items.summary: HDFC Bank plans to raise at least $500 million via dollar bonds this week, utilizing the central bank's subsidized hedging window. The five-year bond issue, with initial guidance of 5-year U.S. Treasury yield plus 120 basis points, is expected to see strong demand. Proceeds will fund foreign branches and subsidiaries, and general corporate purposes.

items.title: US Stock Market: Warsh faces key test as new Fed chair amid inflation concerns
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh faces his first major policy test as inflation remains elevated and markets await clues on future interest rates. The Fed is expected to keep rates unchanged, but investors will closely watch Warsh’s guidance, economic projections, and approach to balancing inflation control, growth concerns, and political pressures.

items.title: Suzlon Energy shares jump over 6%. Why brokerages call it India’s most investible wind energy stock?
items.summary: Suzlon Energy shares rose sharply on Tuesday after the company outlined an ambitious FY31 roadmap focused on expanding beyond wind energy into a broader renewable energy platform. Brokerages including Motilal Oswal, JM Financial, Systematix and Centrum remain bullish, citing Suzlon’s market leadership, execution strength and long-term growth potential.

items.title: Multibagger stock under ₹50 jumps 5% following Dalal Street rally
items.summary: Sindhu Trade Links shares rose 8% to ₹27.06, buoyed by gains in Indian markets amid positive news on US-Iran relations and crude oil prices. The stock has shown strong performance recently, achieving a 664% increase over five years despite a mixed financial outlook for FY26.

items.title: Expert View: Vikas Khemani shares pitfalls of global investing, says large-cap trade is overcrowded
items.summary: Vikas Khemani expressed optimism that FIIs will return to Indian markets because fundamentals demand representation in any serious global portfolio. The retail SIP investor who stays the course benefits from stability of flows and attractive re-entry of FIIs, he added.

items.title: Bank of Japan hikes rates to 1%, highest since 1995, as yen and inflation worries take hold
items.summary: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.

items.title: Japan raises interest rate to highest since 1995
items.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium, Oil & Gas and Power shares fall up to 5% on Day 2. What should investors do?
items.summary: Shares of newly demerged Vedanta entities saw a dip post-listing. Investors are now weighing long-term opportunities, with Vedanta Aluminium emerging as a clear heavyweight due to its scale and expansion plans. Analysts favour aluminium as the top growth bet, while Vedanta Oil & Gas and Power present different investment profiles.

items.title: Lighthouse, Multiples, Motilal PE eye minority stake in safety gear maker Karam
items.summary: Karam is in talks to raise $70-100 million from private equity investors at a valuation of up to $600 million, as interest grows in profitable manufacturing businesses.

items.title: Treasuries shrug at the ceasefire
items.summary: Plus, tech sells bonds because they can

items.title: GIC Re shares tumble 6% as govt launches OFS to divest up to 5% stake
items.summary: The decline came after the company informed stock exchanges on Monday that the govt plans to sell up to 2% stake, equivalent to 3.51 crore shares, through an OFS on June 16-17, 2026

items.title: NBFCs, autos, and multi-decadal themes gain traction as India eyes post–West Asia stability boost: Nitin Raheja
items.summary: Market participants are recalibrating India portfolios towards financials, autos, and long-term structural themes like defence and digital infrastructure as crude prices soften and West Asian tensions ease. Market expert Nitin Raheja of Julius Baer Wealth Advisors sees a cautiously optimistic outlook, with improving medium-term prospects for Indian equities despite near-term volatility.

items.title: US Stock Market: SpaceX IPO sparks debate over index inclusion rules and investor risk
items.summary: SpaceX's market debut sparks debate on how stock indexes should include mega-cap newcomers. Nasdaq added the company, while S&P 500 held back, highlighting differing approaches to growth versus conservatism. This divergence could impact investor returns as more AI giants prepare to list, raising questions about market representation and risk management.

items.title: DLF, Godrej Properties, and other realty stocks jump up to 13% in 3 days. Is the rally sustainable?
items.summary: Real estate stocks have surged nearly 9% in three days, with some analysts citing attractive valuations after a prolonged correction. This rebound is driven by stable operational performance and positive pre-sales guidance, though some advise caution, emphasizing quality-led trades over broad sector bets.

items.title: Falling crude, stronger rupee could boost earnings outlook for India: Deepak Shenoy
items.summary: Indian stocks are poised for a favorable period. Falling crude oil prices and a strengthening rupee offer support. Macroeconomic conditions are improving. While global markets face AI valuation worries, India presents a more attractive landscape. Investors can look forward to potential gains in sectors like metals and healthcare. This outlook suggests a positive trajectory for Indian equities.

items.title: $320 billion danger: Jefferies warns 70 large EM funds are underweight India
items.summary: Despite record FII outflows from Indian equities since September 2024, Jefferies said India remains a consensus underweight among emerging market investors. Its analysis of 70 EM funds managing $320 billion showed 61% remain underweight India. Concerns over rich valuations versus growth and uncertainty around the AI/DRAM cycle may delay foreign inflows, though interest is shifting to hard-asset themes.

items.title: Qualcomm CEO says AI agents will replace apps — as chip giant works on 40 new AI-powered devices
items.summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said he is bullish on smart glasses which could eventually become as big as the smartphone.

items.title: Biotech IPO window is opening but big pharma M&A still sets the pace, top bankers tell CNBC
items.summary: Biopharma dealmaking is driven by pressure to top up drug pipelines ahead of major patent expirations later this decade.

items.title: Asian markets today: Japan's Nikkei surges to 70,000 mark for the first time after BoJ rate hike
items.summary: Japan’s benchmark Nikkei index climbed above the 70,000 level for the first time on Tuesday, 16 June.

items.title: Silver snaps 3-day gaining streak; gold at Rs 1.52 lakh/10 grams as investors eye Iran-US peace deal details, key levels to watch
items.summary: Gold and silver prices snapped their three-day winning streak on MCX as investors tracked developments in the US-Iran peace agreement. Domestic futures slipped marginally, while global gold held steady near recent highs. Analysts expect volatility driven by crude oil, dollar index movements and US Federal Reserve policy signals.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | Quality has rarely been this cheap relative to junk; 3 themes to buy now: Saurabh Mukherjea
items.summary: Quality stocks in India and globally are at their cheapest in years. Saurabh Mukherjea highlights export-oriented manufacturing, European and US small and mid-cap stocks, and Indian financial services as key opportunities. This shift occurs as a prolonged economic stress period begins, favoring quality assets over lower-quality ones. Investors can find compelling value now.

items.title: US Stock Market: Energy stocks slide as oil prices tumble on US-Iran peace breakthrough
items.summary: US energy stocks tumbled Monday as crude oil prices plunged following a breakthrough US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and stabilize global energy routes. The deal eased fears of Strait of Hormuz disruptions, leading to a sharp decline in oil prices and impacting major energy producers and refiners worldwide.

items.title: Brent crude slips as markets await clarity on US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: At 10.06 am on Tuesday, August Brent oil futures were at $82.92, down by 0.30%, and July crude oil futures on WTI were at $80.65, down by 0.12%

items.title: Behind India's Rs 5.5 lakh crore FII selloff lies a hidden list of 84 multibagger winners
items.summary: Foreign institutional investors have sold Indian stocks worth Rs 5.5 lakh crore. However, a hidden list reveals 84 stocks where FIIs have actually increased their holdings. These companies have delivered multibagger returns over two years. Experts suggest this indicates a rotation of funds, not a complete exit from India. Investors are advised to accumulate stocks for future gains.

items.title: HCL Technologies share emerges top gainer on Nifty 50 and Sensex after Sarvam AI investment move
items.summary: HCL Technologies share price has gained 12% in one month, but has risen only 1% in six months. The largecap IT stock has fallen 13% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, and has declined 8% in one year. HCL Tech shares have jumped 51% in three years and 107% in five years.

items.title: Crude oil price steadies after biggest drop in two weeks; Morgan Stanley cuts forecast
items.summary: US-Iran peace deal: MCX crude oil prices rose marginally to ₹7,539 per barrel. Here's what's driving crude oil prices on Tuesday, along with experts' opinions on near-term outlook.

items.title: Beyond Vedanta: The other Anil Agarwal stock that just exploded 500% on AI boom
items.summary: Sterlite Technologies, a Vedanta-backed optical fibre manufacturer, has witnessed an extraordinary 500% surge in its stock value in 2026, driven by the global AI boom. This rally is fueled by the escalating demand for high-speed connectivity infrastructure, particularly for data centers.

items.title: Dalal Street extends rally on softer crude: Sensex up 400 points, Nifty above 23,950; metal stocks lag
items.summary: BSE Sensex climbed 411.62 points or 0.54% to 76,675.95 at 9.32 am after opening at 76,526.77; NSE Nifty 50 increased by 98.70 points or 0.41% to 23,952.60 after opening at 23,923.90

items.title: Rupee rises 5 paise to 94.53 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: On Monday, the rupee strengthened 60 paise to settle at 94.58 against the US dollar

items.title: Vedanta demerger stocks trade mixed: Iron and Steel surges; Aluminium Metal, Oil and Gas shares hit lower circuits
items.summary: Shares of Vedanta Ltd and its demerged entities showed mixed trading following their debut. While some businesses faced profit booking and fell, others like Vedanta Iron & Steel Ltd gained, indicating varied investor reactions to the restructuring exercise.

items.title: Buyback alert! Last date to buy Cyient shares to participate in Rs 720 crore share buyback. Do you own?
items.summary: Cyient’s Rs 720 crore share buyback enters the final stage, with June 16 as the last date to buy shares to qualify. The record date is June 17. The company plans to repurchase shares at Rs 1,125 apiece, offering a premium, while analysts remain cautious on growth outlook.

items.title: Motilal Oswal Financial Services among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Motilal Oswal Financial Services was among five NSE F&O stocks witnessing a sharp rise in futures open interest on June 15. The overall OI increase exceeded 7%, indicating fresh long or short build-up. Other stocks showing notable OI gains included Radico Khaitan, Bajaj Auto, Cochin Shipyard and Tata Power.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | FIIs haven't abandoned India, they've just reshuffled: Samir Arora
items.summary: Foreign investors are not leaving India. They are shifting investments from top companies to other stocks. This rotation involves billions of dollars. Large companies saw selling, while mid-cap and growth-focused firms attracted new capital. This indicates a preference for growth over traditional value. The overall participation in Indian companies has also increased.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei hits 70,000 for the first time after BOJ rate hike
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei stock index reached an all-time high today. This surge followed the Bank of Japan's widely anticipated interest rate hike. The central bank's decision did not signal immediate further tightening. The yen saw a slight strengthening against the dollar. Japanese government bonds experienced a dip. Advantest and Fujikura were among the top performers.

items.title: Global Markets: Chinese government bonds gain global appeal as investors seek diversification amid market volatility
items.summary: Chinese government bonds are attracting growing interest from global investors as rising geopolitical tensions, particularly the Iran conflict, fuel volatility in global fixed-income markets. Unlike sovereign bond yields in the US, Europe, Britain and Japan, which have climbed sharply since March, Chinese government bond yields have fallen, underscoring their relative stability.

items.title: China economy weakens further in May as retail sales post first drop in over three years
items.summary: China's retail sales fell for the first time in more than three years in May, while urban investment contracted more than expected, adding to signs the economic slump deepened.

items.title: NSE likely to file DRHP for IPO by Thursday, eyes ₹5 lakh crore valuation, suggests report
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange is preparing for its IPO, with plans to file draft papers this week. Sebi has granted in-principle approval, clearing a significant hurdle. The NSE aims for a valuation of ₹5-5.25 lakh crore.

items.title: China’s Property Stocks Tumble Back to Pre-2024 Stimulus Levels
items.summary: Chinese property stocks have slid back to levels seen before a raft of stimulus measures from the authorities in September 2024 drove a market turnaround, underscoring lingering pessimism over the sector.
